search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG106 chronic heart failure recommendations follow-up
site:england.nhs.uk heart failure pathway worsening symptoms PDF
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Dose-bearing treatment content was not mapped.
Product guidance does not support a broad follow-up workflow.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for adults with chronic heart failure; not exact for children or every acute decompensation setting.
setting_applicability: Direct for primary, community, and specialist review; urgent deterioration and treatment remain clinician-led.
UAE_applicability: International NICE and regional NHS guidance requires UAE clinician review, local referral pathways, and local prescribing governance.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG106, last updated 2025-09-03, and the official NHS-hosted pathway were opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: NICE NG106 remains current; no newer official copy of the regional supporting pathway was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped follow-up, medication review, and arranged follow-up or referral documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports regular primary-care review, record updates, medicine review, functional assessment, deterioration review, follow-up planning, and specialist access.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped ECG, oxygen, weight, natriuretic peptide, renal, electrolytes, blood count, echocardiogram, and chest-X-ray review.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinical history and examination, NT-proBNP, echocardiography, ECG, chest X-ray, renal, thyroid, liver, lipid, glucose, blood count, and urinalysis review.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-documented medication review without selecting a medicine or dose.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led medication review and monitoring of renal function, electrolytes, blood pressure, pulse, and ECG without autonomous prescribing or dose output.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped follow-up presentation, medication adherence, monitoring, safety, and review fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports review of functional capacity, fluid status, rhythm, cognition, nutrition, medication, renal function, haemoglobin or iron status, deterioration, monitoring frequency, and support needs.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped dyspnoea, orthopnoea or PND, oedema, weight change, examination, presets, prompts, and history.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports worsening breathlessness, orthopnoea or paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnoea, weight increase, oedema or abdominal distension, blood pressure, heart rate, oxygen saturation, JVP, pulmonary findings, and peripheral oedema documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped worsening-heart-failure test fields already covered by NICE.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-selected full blood count, renal and electrolyte tests, natriuretic peptide, liver and thyroid tests, ECG, and chest X-ray review.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-decided safety-netting and referral documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documentation of high-risk deterioration and clinician-led urgent referral or escalation without mapping dose-bearing treatment content.

unresolved_source_gaps: Children, acute treatment, salt or fluid advice, diuretic plans, broad clinician plans, and specific medication doses remain unsupported.
Chest-pain and syncope negatives are not mapped from the heart-failure-specific sections.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
